protocol.definition: An agreed-upon set of rules for how computers talk to each other. Like a handshake means "hello."

tcp.definition: Transmission Control Protocol. A protocol that guarantees data arrives complete and in order. Like a courier who gets a signature. Slower but reliable.

udp.definition: User Datagram Protocol. A protocol that sends data as fast as possible with no checks. Like throwing paper airplanes over a fence. Fast but some might not make it.

ssh.definition: Secure Shell. A way to securely control another computer through text commands. Encrypted from end to end.

http.definition: HyperText Transfer Protocol. The protocol your browser uses to load websites. Not encrypted — anyone on the same network can see what you're browsing.

https.definition: HTTP wrapped in encryption (TLS). Snoopers see gibberish instead of your passwords or credit card numbers.

ip-address.definition: Your computer's unique home address on the internet. Data gets sent to this address like mail to a house.

localhost.definition: Your own computer, and only yours. When a program listens on localhost, nobody else on the network can reach it.

port.definition: A specific door on your computer for a specific program. Port 80 is the door for web traffic, port 22 is the door for SSH.

dns.definition: Domain Name System. The internet's phonebook. You type "google.com", DNS looks up the actual IP address so your browser knows where to go.

brute-force.definition: Guessing passwords over and over really fast until one works. Like trying every key on a keyring until the door opens.

firewall.definition: A filter between your computer and the internet. It decides which ports are open and which stay locked shut.

nmap.definition: Network Mapper. The industry-standard tool for scanning which ports are open on a machine. Used by both defenders and attackers.

rdp.definition: Remote Desktop Protocol. Lets you control a Windows computer from far away as if you were sitting in front of it. A massive target for attackers — never expose port 3389 to the internet.
